sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, dated October 6, 1929
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel praises Hammer’s portrait of Schenker effusively. – In Leipzig he is learning Italian. – He hopes that Hoboken and Vrieslander have patched up their differences. – He is pessimistic about the political and financial situation in Germany, where “Americanism” is working its way into all aspects of society; support for culture has been curtailed; the universities have been affected too; he blames the decline on socialism.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for October 8, 1929: “Von Oppel (Br.): dankt fast überschwänglich für das Bild” (“From Oppel (letter): he thanks me almost gushingly for the portrait”). — Several paragraph-breaks are editorial, guided by emdashes in the original text.

tei:div.tei:p:
das BildThe mezzotint portrait of Schenker made by Viktor Hammer in 1925. lag auf meinem Schreibtisch, als ich Donnerstag nacht hier ankam. Es machte mir eine Riesenfreude, und Ulrich u. Walter finden es ebenso lebendig und ausdrucksvoll und wahr und charakteristisch wie ich. Hammer hat entschieden damit ein Meisterstück geliefert. Besonders vortrefflich ist ihm Ihre sinnig-beschauliche, abwartende Haltung gelungen, ganz Natur. Und diese treff liche ende Ausdruck der Augen, die den Dingen bis in die dunkelsten Tiefen schauen bei aller S Ruhe des sicher abwägenden Geistes. Jeder Strich an seinem Platze, fabelhaft die Verteilung von Licht u. Schatten, die ganze Komposition in der Form schön, unbeschreiblich schön, alle Details glänzend gelöst. Ich danke ihm und Ihnen
allerherzlichst für diese herrliche Gabe, das Bild kommt morgen zum Rahmen, u. dann kommt es über meinen Schreibtisch. Eine grössere Freude könnten Sie mir gar nicht machen. –

Den Jungen geht’s gut, sie haben grade Herbstferien, u. bis auf Walter, leidliche Zeugnisse. Wir schwimmen noch, aber morgen ist leider Schluss damit, da die Badeanstalt wegen Strassenbau abgebrochen wird. Das Wetter ist noch sehr milde, doch herbstelt es schon stark.

In Leipzig feierten wir Klengels 70. Geburtstag,Julius Klengel (1859–1933), German cellist, born in Leipzig, principal cellist of the Gewandhaus Orchestra and member of the Gewandhaus Quartet, also concert soloist, professor at the Leipzig Conservatory, and composer of many works involving the cello. er ist ein prachtvoller Mensch u. Kollege und versteht sein Cello. –

Ich lerne jetzt in einem Kurs bei uns italienisch mit, bei einem Kollegen Smigelski, der früher in Italien Mönch war, dann aus dem Kloster ging u. Musikant wurde.Ernst Richard Edmund Smigelski (1881–1950), born in Silesia (southern Poland), took holy orders in Rome in 1895 and spent some time in India before giving up monastic life to study theory and composition at the Leipzig Conservatory. He served as conductor at the opera house in Vilnius and was a critic for Leipzig newspapers before taking up a post at the Conservatory, which included the teaching of Italian. –

Was ist nun mit Vrieslander? Hat er eine Tätigkeit? Und wie hat sich A. v. H zu den Dingen gestellt? Hoffentlich findet letzterer den guten
Weg eines vernünftigen Einklanges zurück. Denn was nutzt ihm sein vieles Geld, wo seine Seele leer ausgeht? –

Wir stehen in Deutschland vor einem schlimmen Winter: überall drohen Arbeiterentlassungen aus Mangel an Aufträgen. Dazu diese ewigen Finanzskandale, Frankfurter Versicherungskonzern,The Frankfurter Allgemeine Versicherung was a huge insurance corporation which assumed control of several smaller insurance companies that had fallen victim to the hyperinflation of the 1920s. In 1929 it was investigated on account of reports of irregular business practices, which were found to be true; the company was dissolved and subsequently taken over by other insurance concerns. Raiffeisenbank, Berlin-Sklarek.In the “Sklarek Scandal,” the city of Berlin was defrauded of an estimated 11 million marks by three Jewish brothers, Leo, Max, and Willy Sklarek, who also attempted to bribe municipal officials to cover up their illicit activities; they were arrested in 1929. The legal action against them spanned more than two years and is documented in over 2,000 pages of court records. The National Socialists used the case to discredit the Weimar Republic, and Jewish people more generally. Willi died in 1938; Leo and Max were murdered in Nazi concentration camps in 1942 and 1944, respectively. Der Amerikanismus macht leider auf der ganzen Linie böse Fortschritte. Unsern Industrieherren fehlt es auch durchaus an dem guten Willen, die wirklich vorhanden sozialen Härten auszugleichen, verärgert darüber, dass der Staat durch seine falsche Steuerpolitik jede Neubildung von Kapital verhindert. Man muss tatsächlich froh sein, wenn man jetzt Arbeit hat und sich geradeso durchschlägt. Schlimm ist geradezu der Abbau i. d. geistigen Betrieben, wie Sie aus d. beilieg. Artikel aus d. DAZ: Kulturabbau entnehmen können. – Auch bei den Universitäten wird stark gestrichen!
Das sind die Folgen dieses verbohrten Socialismus, der die blöde Masse zum Götzen erhob. –

Übrigens noch eine erfreuliche Nachricht: ich hörte dieser Tage, dass so ziemlich alles, auch s. direkten Anbringer, von Herrn Jöde abrückt, langsam kommt die Jugend hinter die Hohlheit dieser Scheingrössen. –

tei:closer.tei:salute: u. herzliche Grüsse Ihnen beiden von uns allen!

tei:div.tei:p:
Your portrait was lying on my desk when I returned here on Thursday night. It gave me a great deal of joy, and Ulrich and Walter find it just as animated and expressive, and true and characteristic, as I do. Hammer has decisively delivered a masterpiece. He has succeeded in a specially admirable way in conveying your thoughtfully contemplative, expectant bearing. And this trenchant expression of your eyes, which observes things to their darkest depths with all the quiet of a confidently appraising intellect. Every stroke is in place, the distribution of light and shade is fabulous, the entire composition beautiful – indescribably beautiful – in its form, all details radiantly resolved. I thank him and you
in the most heartfelt way for this splendid gift; the portrait will go to be framed tomorrow, and then it will sit above my desk. You could not possibly have given me a greater pleasure.

The boys are well; their autumn holiday has just begun and, apart from Walter, their school grades are passable. We have been swimming until now; but tomorrow that will unfortunately come to an end, as the pool is closed on account of road works. The weather is still very mild but it is already turning quite autumnal.
In Leipzig we celebrated Klengel’s 70th birthday. He is a splendid person and colleague, and he understands the cello.
I am studying Italian in a course here, with a colleague named Smigelski, who was previously a monk in Italy and then left the convent and became a musician. –

What’s the news with Vrieslander? Does he have a position? And how did Anthony van Hoboken respond to those things? I hope that the latter will find the
path back to a sensible harmoniousness. For of what use is his vast amount of money if his soul comes away empty-handed?

In Germany, we are about to face a bad winter: workers are threatened with layoff everywhere, on account of a shortage of contracts. Added to which these eternal financial scandals, the Frankfurt Insurance Concern, the Raiffeisenbank, Berlin-Sklarek. The American Way is, unfortunately, taking evil strides everywhere. Our captains of industry are utterly lacking in the goodwill needed to compensate for the social hardships that are really at hand; this, compounded by the state on account of the wrong taxation policies, thwarts every attempt at regenerating capital. One must be truly glad if one has work now and can just about manage things. The near-disassembly in the intellectual fields is bad, as you can gather from reading the enclosed article from the Deutsche Allgemeine Zeitung: “The Dismantling of Culture.” In the universities, too, there have been severe cutbacks!
These are the consequences of this pig-headed socialism, which the stupid masses raised to a false god.

Incidentally, a piece of good news. I have heard these days that pretty much everyone, even his closest adherents, is turning away from Mr. Jöde; the youth are gradually discovering the hollowness of this sham bigwig.
And now, once again, heartfelt thanks ...

tei:closer.tei:salute: and cordial greetings to the two of you
